sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, presented with a complaint of neck pain following a car accident one week prior, where he was hit from behind and his head and neck moved forward rapidly.
He describes the pain as a 7/10, which is exacerbated by movement of the neck, particularly flexion and extension.
The pain is localized to the back of the neck and has not been alleviated significantly by ibuprofen.
Past medical history: The patient has no prior history of neck injuries or conditions, but has a history of playing football years ago without any issues.
Review of systems: The patient denies any headaches, hearing problems, or visual disturbances.
Current medications: The patient is currently taking ibuprofen as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.

Physical exam: The patient exhibited positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, positive pain with flexion, severe pain with extension, positive pain with rotation, and positive pain with lateral bending.
Test Results: The patient's x-ray results showed no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has neck strain causing his neck pain.
Problem list: Neck strain; suspected whiplash; unknown concussion status.
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for anti-inflammatory purposes.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to avoid straining his neck and to relax his neck movements.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any potential nerve impingement.
I referred the patient to either physical rehabilitation or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment once the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: I will review the MRI results and follow up with the patient to discuss further treatment options.
